tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: 100 Strangers Honor Navy Veteran With No Family at His Funeral
stories.summary: Nearly 100 strangers crossed state lines to ensure 82-year-old Navy veteran John Mulligan wasn't buried alone, with Tammy Jo Cormier driving over an hour to honor his service.

stories.headline: Man Paralyzed by ALS Speaks Clearly Again Through Brain Implant
stories.summary: Casey Harrell, paralyzed by ALS, clocked over 3,800 hours using his brain implant to speak, work, and read to his young daughter—becoming the first 'power user' of this life-changing device.

stories.headline: Displaced Lebanese Grandmother Feeds Thousands From Her Tent Bakery
stories.summary: Soubhiye Zeiter, a 63-year-old grandmother displaced by war, bakes mana'eesh daily in her tent bakery to feed thousands of neighbors in Beirut, greeting people with coffee and boundless warmth.

stories.headline: Late-Blooming Goaltender Brandon Bussi Carries Hurricanes to Stanley Cup Glory
stories.summary: Journeyman goaltender Brandon Bussi stepped in mid-series to backstop Carolina to their first Stanley Cup in 20 years, proving dreams come true at any age.

stories.headline: Good Samaritan Drives Stranger Home After Fortunate Hospital Name Mix-Up
stories.summary: Richard Hayes, a local stranger, drove 86-year-old Norman Camburn home from hospital after a fortunate name mix-up, refusing thanks and humbly saying 'you give a hand where you can.'

stories.headline: 40-Year-Old Cape Verde Goalkeeper Delivers Career-Defining Performance in World Cup Debut
stories.summary: At 40 years old, Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha made his World Cup debut with seven stunning saves, holding tournament favorite Spain to a 0-0 draw in his nation's historic first match.

stories.headline: South Boston Community Floods Lemonade Stand to Support Children Robbed at Gunpoint
stories.summary: Hundreds of South Boston neighbors flooded a lemonade stand fundraiser organized by Nicole Raukohl to support two children robbed at gunpoint, turning trauma into community triumph.

stories.headline: Rare Red-Headed Woodpeckers Return to Ontario's Rice Lake Plains After 20 Years
stories.summary: After over two decades of restoration work, endangered Red-headed Woodpeckers have returned to Ontario's Rice Lake Plains, reclaiming their native tallgrass prairie and oak savanna.

stories.headline: One-Time CRISPR Treatment Leaves 62% of Patients Attack-Free
stories.summary: In a groundbreaking Phase III trial, a one-time CRISPR therapy left 62% of hereditary angioedema patients attack-free, marking the first in vivo gene-editing treatment nearing market approval.

stories.headline: Brain Implant Helps Parkinson's Patients Walk Freely Again
stories.summary: UC San Francisco researchers developed an adaptive brain implant that adjusts stimulation within fractions of a second as Parkinson's patients walk, dramatically reducing falls and freezing of gait.

stories.headline: Ancient Lake Sturgeon Species Shows Signs of Recovery
stories.summary: Lake sturgeon reintegration efforts show promising early results as researchers monitor the recovery of this ancient fish species in its native waters.
